meaning: n.一国的政治上或经济上的孤立主义
examples: Isolationism is also on the rise.
Isolationism is trying to police the world but creating more resentment than gratitude.
He warned that it would be a misinterpretation if anyone saw the US withdrawal as a move towards isolationism.
Isolationism of each variety can be found in both political parties, but isolationism is witless.
" A fuming Clinton, meanwhile, was blasting the Republicans for their "reckless partisanship" and "neo-isolationism.
